geographicSummary: District One covers the Far North, Far Northwest, and Northland neighborhoods of Columbus. Its boundaries run roughly from Sawmill Road to the west, Route 161 to the south, Cleveland Avenue to the east, and the Polaris area to the north. The district is unique in that its neighborhoods are highly diverse in size and demographic makeup, and its poverty rate is among the lowest of the nine districts.

pointsOfInterest.name: Polaris Fashion Place
pointsOfInterest.description: Large indoor shopping and entertainment complex anchoring the district's northern commercial corridor.

pointsOfInterest.name: Highbanks Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 1,204-acre park along the Olentangy River with hiking trails, nature center, sledding hill, and canoe access.

pointsOfInterest.name: Topgolf Columbus
pointsOfInterest.description: Three-story entertainment venue with hitting bays, restaurant, and event spaces near Polaris.

pointsOfInterest.name: Lazelle Woods Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Neighborhood park in Northland with walking trails, sports courts, and playground facilities.

pointsOfInterest.name: Northland Community Library
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus Metropolitan Library branch serving the Northland neighborhood with programs and meeting spaces.

pointsOfInterest.name: IKEA Columbus
pointsOfInterest.description: Major home furnishings retailer in the Polaris commercial district.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (backfill_2021.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
